query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "mobilegym: a verifiable and highly parallel simulation platform for mo" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on discrete event and agent-based simulation for human-centric retail systems (fitting rooms), which is an off-domain application compared to the user's question about "MobileGym," a verifiable simulation platform for mobile robotics or autonomous agents. While both involve simulation, the paper does not address the specific mechanism, domain (mobile robotics/LLM agents), or canonical methodology of the MobileGym platform, failing to meet any of the inclusion criteria (a-f).
